items.correct: No — felt is non-woven; loose fibres are matted together with moisture and friction.
items.misconception: Yes — all fabric is made by weaving threads.
items.random_wrong: Yes — felt is knitted from thick yarn.
items.why: Students assume every textile is woven, but felting bonds fibres directly without any threads crossing.

items.q: In batik, what does the wax do?
items.correct: It resists the dye, so waxed areas keep their original colour.
items.misconception: The wax is the dye and adds the colour.
items.random_wrong: It stiffens the fabric ready for embroidery.
items.why: Students think the wax adds colour, when batik works because wax blocks dye from reaching the cloth.

items.q: What is the difference between warp and weft?
items.correct: Warp threads run lengthwise under tension; the weft is woven across, over and under them.
items.misconception: Warp and weft are two names for the same threads.
items.random_wrong: Warp is the colour and weft is the texture.
items.why: Learners merge the two terms; the warp is fixed on the loom and the weft travels across it.

items.q: In weaving, the threads held under tension running lengthwise on the loom are the:
items.why: Correct answer: Warp. “Selvedge” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.correct: Using wax to resist dye in chosen areas.
items.misconception: Printing with a carved lino block.
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Using wax to resist dye in chosen areas.. “Printing with a carved lino block.” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.correct: Stitching cut fabric shapes onto a base fabric.
items.why: It's Stitching cut fabric shapes onto a base fabric.. “Weaving threads on a loom.” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.correct: Matting wool fibres with warm water, soap and friction.
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Matting wool fibres with warm water, soap and friction.. “Knitting wool with needles.” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: A woven pattern repeat uses 8 warp threads. How many warp threads are needed for 5 repeats?
items.why: Exact answer: 40 threads. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
